title: Lavender Scare purges LGBTQ+ workers from US government
description: During the early Cold War the US federal government began systematically dismissing and barring gay and lesbian employees from the military and civil service, casting them as security risks. The campaign, later called the Lavender Scare, cost thousands their careers.

title: Daughters of Bilitis founded in San Francisco
description: The Daughters of Bilitis was formed in San Francisco as the first lesbian civil and political rights organization in the United States, offering community and advocacy at a time of widespread persecution.

title: José Sarria runs for public office in San Francisco
description: José Sarria ran for the San Francisco Board of Supervisors, becoming one of the first openly gay people to seek elected office in the United States. Although he lost, his candidacy showed a queer electorate could mobilize.

title: Compton's Cafeteria Riot in San Francisco
description: Transgender women and drag queens fought back against police harassment at Compton's Cafeteria in San Francisco's Tenderloin district, an early act of collective trans resistance that helped galvanize community organizing.

title: Stonewall uprising in New York City
description: Patrons of the Stonewall Inn in Greenwich Village resisted a police raid, sparking several nights of protest. The uprising is widely regarded as a turning point for the modern LGBTQ+ rights movement.

title: Kathy Kozachenko wins Ann Arbor council seat
description: Kathy Kozachenko was elected to the Ann Arbor City Council in Michigan, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ person elected to public office in the United States.

title: First out California state legislator elected
description: An openly LGBTQ+ candidate was reported elected to the California state legislature, an early milestone for queer representation at the state level. The specific officeholder is not named in the source.

title: Harvey Milk elected in San Francisco
description: Harvey Milk won a seat on the San Francisco Board of Supervisors, becoming the first openly gay elected official in California. He served until his assassination in 1978 and became an enduring symbol of the movement.

title: US Representative Barney Frank comes out
description: Massachusetts Congressman Barney Frank publicly came out as gay, becoming one of the first members of the US Congress to do so voluntarily while in office.

title: John Laird among first openly gay US mayors
description: John Laird served as an openly gay mayor in California, counted among the first out gay mayors in the United States. Source details on the year and city are inconsistent and warrant review.

title: First openly lesbian judge appointed in the US
description: An openly lesbian judge was appointed to the bench, a first for LGBTQ+ representation in the American judiciary. The source dates the milestone to the 1980s without naming the individual.

title: Deborah Glick elected to New York State Assembly
description: Deborah Glick won a seat in the New York State Assembly, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ person elected to the New York state legislature.

title: Dale McCormick elected to Maine State Senate
description: Dale McCormick was elected to the Maine State Senate, becoming the first openly lesbian person elected to a state senate in the United States.

title: Tom Duane elected to New York City Council
description: Tom Duane won a seat on the New York City Council as an openly gay man living with HIV, disclosing his status during the campaign.

title: Gay & Lesbian Victory Fund founded
description: The Gay & Lesbian Victory Fund was established as a national organization dedicated to recruiting, training and funding openly LGBTQ+ candidates for public office in the United States.

title: Gail Shibley appointed to the Oregon House
description: Gail Shibley was appointed to the Oregon House of Representatives, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ member of the Oregon legislature.

title: Glen Maxey wins full term in Texas House
description: Glen Maxey won a full term in the Texas House of Representatives as the first openly gay member of the Texas Legislature.

title: Tammy Baldwin wins Wisconsin Assembly seat
description: Tammy Baldwin was elected to the Wisconsin State Assembly as an openly gay candidate, beginning a political career that would later carry her to Congress and the US Senate.

title: Victory Fund backs first US congressional candidates
description: The Victory Fund supported its first slate of openly LGBTQ+ candidates for the US Congress, extending its work from local and state races to the federal level.

title: Roberta Achtenberg confirmed by the US Senate
description: Roberta Achtenberg was confirmed by the US Senate as an Assistant Secretary at the Department of Housing and Urban Development, becoming the first openly lesbian official confirmed to a federal post.

title: Minnesota adds sexual orientation to Human Rights Act
description: Minnesota amended its Human Rights Act to prohibit discrimination based on sexual orientation, a change championed by openly gay state senator Allan Spear.

title: LGBTQ+ Victory Institute founded
description: The Victory Institute was established to train openly LGBTQ+ leaders and support them through campaigns and public service, complementing the electoral work of the Victory Fund.

title: Sheila Kuehl elected to the California Assembly
description: Sheila Kuehl won election to the California State Assembly, becoming the first openly gay or lesbian person elected to the California Legislature.

title: Congressman Steve Gunderson outed
description: Wisconsin Congressman Steve Gunderson was publicly outed as gay while serving in the US House, making him one of the few openly gay Republicans in Congress at the time.

title: Tim Van Zandt elected to the Missouri House
description: Tim Van Zandt won a seat in the Missouri House of Representatives as an openly gay candidate, an early milestone for LGBTQ+ representation in the state.

title: Michael Nelson elected mayor of Carrboro
description: Michael Nelson was elected mayor of Carrboro, North Carolina, becoming one of the first openly gay mayors in the state.

title: Roberta Achtenberg runs for San Francisco mayor
description: Roberta Achtenberg mounted an openly lesbian campaign for mayor of San Francisco, one of the highest-profile LGBTQ+ big-city mayoral bids of the decade.

title: Sabrina Sojourner elected DC shadow representative
description: Sabrina Sojourner was elected as a shadow representative for the District of Columbia, becoming one of the first openly lesbian Black women elected to office in the United States.

title: Congressman Jim Kolbe comes out
description: Arizona Congressman Jim Kolbe came out as gay while serving in the US House, becoming an openly gay Republican member of Congress.

title: David Parks elected in Nevada
description: David Parks was elected to the Nevada Legislature, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ person to serve in the state's elected office.

title: Benjamin Cruz appointed to the Guam Supreme Court
description: Benjamin Cruz was appointed to the High Court of Guam as an openly gay justice, a milestone for LGBTQ+ representation in the US territory's judiciary.

title: Philip Reed elected to New York City Council
description: Philip Reed was elected to the New York City Council, becoming the first openly gay Black member of the body.

title: Cathy Woolard elected to Atlanta City Council
description: Cathy Woolard won a seat on the Atlanta City Council, becoming the first openly gay person elected to office in the city.

title: Jackie Biskupski elected in Utah
description: Jackie Biskupski was elected to the Utah Legislature, becoming the first openly gay person elected to state office in Utah.

title: Tammy Baldwin elected to the US Congress
description: Tammy Baldwin of Wisconsin was elected to the US House of Representatives, becoming the first openly gay non-incumbent and the first out woman elected to Congress.

title: Fred Hochberg appointed SBA Deputy Administrator
description: Fred Hochberg was appointed Deputy Administrator of the US Small Business Administration, one of the highest-ranking openly gay federal appointees of the era.

title: Tom Duane becomes New York's first out state senator
description: Tom Duane took office in the New York State Senate, becoming the first openly gay member of that chamber.

title: Jim Hormel sworn in as first openly gay US ambassador
description: James Hormel was sworn in as US Ambassador to Luxembourg, becoming the first openly gay person to represent the United States as an ambassador.

title: Karla Drenner elected in Georgia
description: Karla Drenner was elected to the Georgia General Assembly, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ person to serve in the state legislature.

title: Four lesbian lawmakers elected to California Assembly
description: Four openly lesbian candidates won seats in the California State Assembly in the same cycle, a notable concentration of LGBTQ+ representation in a single legislature.

title: Gay-majority council elected in Wilton Manors
description: Voters in Wilton Manors, Florida, elected an openly gay majority to the city commission, a first for LGBTQ+ political power at the municipal level.

title: Two lesbian lawmakers come out
description: Two sitting lesbian state lawmakers publicly came out during the year, adding to visible LGBTQ+ representation in elected office. The source does not name the individuals or states.

title: Cathy Woolard elected Atlanta council president
description: Cathy Woolard was elected president of the Atlanta City Council, becoming one of the first openly gay people to lead a major US city council.

title: Jarrett Barrios wins Massachusetts Senate seat
description: Jarrett Barrios was elected to the Massachusetts State Senate as an openly gay Latino lawmaker, expanding LGBTQ+ and Latino representation in the chamber.

title: California LGBTQ+ Legislative Caucus founded
description: Openly LGBTQ+ members of the California Legislature formed a legislative caucus to coordinate on policy, the first such body in a US statehouse.

title: David Cicilline elected mayor of Providence
description: David Cicilline was elected mayor of Providence, Rhode Island, becoming the first openly gay mayor of a US state capital.

title: Ron Oden elected first Black openly gay US mayor
description: Ron Oden was elected mayor of Palm Springs, California, becoming the first openly gay Black mayor in the United States. The source's stated location of Kansas appears to be an error.

title: Adam Ebbin elected in Virginia
description: Adam Ebbin was elected to the Virginia House of Delegates, becoming the first openly gay member of the Virginia General Assembly.

title: Chuck Wolfe becomes CEO of the Victory Fund
description: Chuck Wolfe became chief executive of the Victory Fund and Victory Institute, leading the organizations' national campaign and leadership programs.

title: Lupe Valdez elected sheriff in Texas
description: Lupe Valdez was elected sheriff of Dallas County, Texas, becoming the first openly lesbian Latina sheriff in the United States.

title: Rives Kistler joins a state supreme court
description: Rives Kistler became the first openly gay justice to serve on a US state supreme court. The source names North Carolina, but Kistler served on the Oregon Supreme Court.

title: Victory Fund endorses first transgender candidates
description: The Victory Fund endorsed its first openly transgender candidates for public office, extending its support beyond gay, lesbian and bisexual contenders.

title: Latina lesbian elected to San Antonio council
description: An openly lesbian Latina candidate was elected to the San Antonio City Council in Texas, a first for the city. The source does not name the officeholder.

title: First openly LGBTQ+ official elected in Nebraska
description: Nebraska elected its first openly LGBTQ+ official, extending queer representation to the state. The source does not name the individual or office.

title: Victory Institute merges with INLGO
description: The Victory Institute merged with the International Network of Lesbian and Gay Officials, consolidating support and networking for openly LGBTQ+ public officials.

title: Virginia Linder elected to Oregon Supreme Court
description: Virginia Linder won election to the Oregon Supreme Court, becoming one of the first openly LGBTQ+ people elected to a state supreme court.

title: Lesbian legislative candidates advance in the South
description: Openly lesbian candidates broke new ground in Southern state legislative races, expanding LGBTQ+ representation in a historically resistant region. The source gives no names or specific states.

title: Program launched to help officials come out
description: A new program was created to support elected officials who wanted to come out publicly as LGBTQ+ while in office, offering guidance through the process.

title: First out legislators elected in Alabama and Tennessee
description: Openly LGBTQ+ candidates were elected to the state legislatures of Alabama and Tennessee, firsts for both Southern states. The source's description of the candidates warrants review.

title: Out elected officials surpass 400 in the US
description: The number of openly LGBTQ+ elected officials in the United States passed 400, marking sustained growth in queer political representation.

title: New York Assembly passes marriage equality bill
description: The New York State Assembly passed a marriage equality bill, an early legislative step toward opening marriage to same-sex couples. The measure did not become law that year.

title: Kate Brown elected statewide in Oregon
description: Kate Brown was elected Oregon Secretary of State, becoming one of the first openly bisexual people elected to statewide office in the United States.

title: Congressional LGBT Equality Caucus formed
description: Members of the US House of Representatives established the Congressional LGBT Equality Caucus to advance LGBTQ+ rights within Congress.

title: Feature film Milk released
description: The biographical film about San Francisco supervisor Harvey Milk reached cinemas, bringing his life and assassination to a wide audience and renewing attention to LGBTQ+ political history.

title: Annise Parker elected mayor of Houston
description: Annise Parker won the Houston mayoral election, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ mayor of a major US city.

title: First openly LGBTQ+ US Attorney appointed
description: President Obama appointed the first openly LGBTQ+ person to serve as a US Attorney, a milestone for queer representation in federal law enforcement.

title: First Black lesbian state lawmaker elected in Georgia
description: Georgia elected the first openly Black lesbian state legislator in the United States, expanding LGBTQ+ and Black representation in the statehouse.

title: David Cicilline elected to Congress from Rhode Island
description: David Cicilline was elected to the US House of Representatives, becoming Rhode Island's first openly gay member of Congress.

title: Nickie Antonio elected to the Ohio House
description: Nickie Antonio won a seat in the Ohio House of Representatives, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ person elected to the Ohio legislature.

title: Jim Gray elected mayor of Lexington
description: Jim Gray was elected mayor of Lexington, Kentucky, becoming the city's first openly gay mayor.

title: Robin Kniech elected to Denver City Council
description: Robin Kniech won an at-large seat on the Denver City Council, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ member of the council.

title: New York legalizes same-sex marriage
description: New York enacted the Marriage Equality Act, opening civil marriage to same-sex couples. LGBTQ+ lawmakers were central to securing the votes for passage.

title: LaWana Mayfield elected to Charlotte City Council
description: LaWana Mayfield won a seat on the Charlotte City Council in North Carolina, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ member of the body.

title: Four LGBTQ+ candidates elected to US Congress
description: Four openly LGBTQ+ candidates won seats in the US Congress in the same election cycle, a record class for queer federal representation at the time.

title: Tammy Baldwin elected to the US Senate
description: Tammy Baldwin of Wisconsin won election to the US Senate, becoming the first openly gay person elected to the chamber.

title: Openly gay candidate runs for US president
description: An openly gay Republican mounted a campaign for the US presidency, one of the earliest openly gay major-party presidential bids.

title: Delaware senator comes out during marriage debate
description: A Delaware state senator came out publicly during the legislature's debate over marriage equality, adding a personal voice to the vote.

title: Congressman Mike Michaud comes out
description: Maine Congressman Mike Michaud came out as gay while serving in the US House, later running for governor of Maine.

title: Illinois legalizes same-sex marriage
description: Illinois enacted a law opening civil marriage to same-sex couples, signed in November 2013 and taking effect the following year.

title: Toni Atkins becomes California Assembly Speaker
description: Toni Atkins was elevated to Speaker of the California State Assembly, becoming the first openly lesbian person to hold the post.

title: Maura Healey elected Massachusetts attorney general
description: Maura Healey won election as Attorney General of Massachusetts, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ state attorney general in the United States.

title: Robert Garcia elected mayor of Long Beach
description: Robert Garcia was elected mayor of Long Beach, California, becoming the city's first openly gay and first Latino mayor.

title: Jackie Biskupski elected mayor of Salt Lake City
description: Jackie Biskupski was elected mayor of Salt Lake City, Utah, becoming the city's first openly LGBTQ+ mayor.

title: Two LGBTQ+ leaders elected in Nashville
description: Nashville voters elected two openly LGBTQ+ leaders to city office in the same period, broadening queer representation in the Tennessee capital. The source does not name them.

title: Mayor Pete Buttigieg comes out in South Bend
description: Pete Buttigieg, mayor of South Bend, Indiana, came out as gay in a public essay while in office and ahead of his re-election.

title: Kate Brown becomes first out US governor
description: Kate Brown of Oregon won election as governor, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ person elected governor in the United States after first assuming the office through succession.

title: Eric Fanning becomes first openly gay Army secretary
description: Eric Fanning was confirmed as US Secretary of the Army, becoming the first openly gay leader of a US military service branch.

title: Five queer lawmakers of color win elections
description: Five LGBTQ+ candidates of color won legislative races in the same cycle, marking growth in the diversity of queer elected representation. The source does not name the individuals.

title: Jenny Durkan elected mayor of Seattle
description: Jenny Durkan was elected mayor of Seattle, Washington, becoming the city's first openly lesbian mayor.

title: Palm Springs elects first all-LGBTQ+ city council
description: Voters in Palm Springs, California, elected a city council composed entirely of openly LGBTQ+ members, reported as the first such body in the United States.

title: Danica Roem elected in Virginia
description: Danica Roem won a seat in the Virginia House of Delegates, becoming the first openly transgender person elected and seated in a US state legislature.

title: Rainbow Wave marks record LGBTQ+ election gains
description: The 2018 US midterm elections produced record numbers of openly LGBTQ+ winners across federal, state and local offices, an outcome widely called the Rainbow Wave.

title: Jared Polis elected first openly gay US governor
description: Jared Polis won election as governor of Colorado, becoming the first openly gay man elected governor in the United States.

title: Christine Hallquist runs for governor of Vermont
description: Christine Hallquist won a major party's nomination for governor of Vermont, becoming the first openly transgender gubernatorial nominee of a major US party.

title: Lori Lightfoot elected mayor of Chicago
description: Lori Lightfoot was elected mayor of Chicago, becoming the city's first openly LGBTQ+ mayor and its first Black woman to hold the office.

title: Out candidates gain ground in Nashville
description: Openly LGBTQ+ candidates expanded their presence in Nashville politics during the year. The source gives no names or specific offices.

title: LGBTQ+ representation grows in Indianapolis
description: The number of openly LGBTQ+ officials in Indianapolis government rose sharply during the year. The source gives no names or specific offices.

title: Pete Buttigieg wins Iowa presidential caucus
description: Pete Buttigieg won the Iowa Democratic caucuses, becoming the first openly gay candidate to win a state presidential nominating contest.

title: First Black out members of Congress elected in New York
description: New York voters elected the first openly LGBTQ+ Black members of the US Congress, expanding both queer and Black representation on Capitol Hill.

title: Transgender candidates win in three new states
description: Openly transgender candidates were elected to office in three US states for the first time in the same cycle, including a state senate win in Delaware.

title: Pete Buttigieg confirmed as Transportation Secretary
description: Pete Buttigieg was confirmed by the US Senate as Secretary of Transportation, becoming the first openly gay person confirmed to a presidential Cabinet post.

title: Rachel Levine confirmed as first out trans federal official
description: Dr. Rachel Levine was confirmed by the US Senate as Assistant Secretary for Health, becoming the first openly transgender official confirmed by the Senate.

title: Karine Jean-Pierre becomes White House press secretary
description: Karine Jean-Pierre became White House Press Secretary, the first openly LGBTQ+ person and first Black woman to hold the role.

skip_reason: not-lgbtq-history — the Dobbs decision overturning Roe v. Wade concerns abortion rights, not LGBTQ+ history

title: US Congress passes the Respect for Marriage Act
description: The US Congress passed the Respect for Marriage Act, which requires federal and interstate recognition of same-sex and interracial marriages. It was signed into law in December 2022.

title: Laphonza Butler sworn into the US Senate
description: Laphonza Butler was sworn into the US Senate representing California, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ Black woman to serve in the chamber.

title: LGBTQ+ candidates post strong 2023 election results
description: Openly LGBTQ+ candidates won a notable set of races in the 2023 off-year elections, described by advocates as another Rainbow Wave. The source gives no specific names.

title: First out legislator elected in Mississippi
description: Mississippi elected its first openly LGBTQ+ state legislator, extending queer representation to the statehouse. The source names only a surname, so details warrant review.

title: Sarah McBride elected first transgender US House member
description: Sarah McBride of Delaware was elected to the US House of Representatives, becoming the first openly transgender person elected to the US Congress.

title: Julie Johnson brings out representation to the US South
description: Julie Johnson of Texas was elected to the US House of Representatives, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ member of Congress from the US South.

title: Emily Randall elected first LGBTQ+ Latina in Congress
description: Emily Randall of Washington state was elected to the US House of Representatives, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ Latina member of Congress.

title: Gina Ortiz Jones elected first out mayor of San Antonio
description: Gina Ortiz Jones was elected mayor of San Antonio, Texas, becoming the city's first openly LGBTQ+ mayor.

title: First gay man elected to the Delaware Senate
description: Delaware elected its first openly gay man to the state senate, adding to LGBTQ+ representation in the legislature. The source does not name the individual.

title: Transgender candidate elected to Ohio city council
description: An openly transgender candidate was elected to a city council in Ohio, a local milestone for trans representation. The source does not name the individual or city.

title: Erica Deuso sworn in as first transgender mayor
description: Erica Deuso was sworn in as mayor in Pennsylvania, reported as the state's first openly transgender mayor. Details of the recent event warrant review.
